74330.subject: 🔷 The Daily: House crypto tax hearing divide
74330.body:
CFTC unveils sweeping rule proposal for fast-growing prediction markets | Bitcoin Layer 2 Botanix to wind down network, urges users to withdraw assets | Japan's three megabanks to debut live stablecoin transactions by March 2027 | More than 50% of bitcoin supply is underwater; prior bottoms followed within weeks, often after a final leg lower: K33 |

The Daily ( https://www.theblock.co/?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

Written by James Hunt | Edited by Kyle Baird

Presented by

LMAX ( https://www.theblock.co/lmax?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

Happy Wednesday! *Bitcoin* is hovering near cycle lows as ETF outflows mount and rate fears linger, with analysts at *CoinShares* , *QCP Capital* , and *Capital.com* pointing ( https://www.theblock.co/post/404237/in-the-shadow-of-geopolitics-and-ai-bitcoin-hovers-near-cycle-lows-as-etf-outflows-and-rate-fears-deepen-worst-stretch-of-2026?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) to a toxic mix of geopolitical tensions, hawkish *Fed* expectations, and capital flowing toward AI-linked trades.

In today's newsletter, a *House* committee debates how to *tax digital assets* , the *CFTC* introduces a slate of new rules for *prediction markets* , *Botanix* is winding down its *Bitcoin* Layer 2 network, and more.

Meanwhile, *Ethereum* could become a fully zero-knowledge proof-based protocol in 3 to 5 years, according to *Joe Lubin* ( https://www.theblock.co/post/404185/ethereum-fully-zero-knowledge-proof-based-protocol-3-to-5-years-joe-lubin?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ).

Plus, *Fold* shares jump 160% ( https://www.theblock.co/post/404272/fold-discloses-45-million-bitcoin-sale-pays-off-collateralized-debt-in-full-shares-surge-160?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) after disclosing a $45 million *bitcoin* sale and paying off its collateralized debt in full.

*P.S.* Don't forget to check out The Funding , a biweekly rundown of crypto VC trends. It's a great read — and just like The Daily , it's free to subscribe ( https://www.theblock.co/newsletters?select=The%20Funding&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) !

*THE BIG ONE*

*🏛️ House crypto tax hearing reveals divide over urgency in advancing legislation*

( https://www.theblock.co/post/404187/house-crypto-tax-hearing-legislation?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

The *House Ways and Means Committee* held its first digital asset tax hearing in years as lawmakers debated how to build a framework for taxing crypto.

* Republicans recently introduced seven crypto tax bills covering

74328.subject: Mt. Gox Ghost Returns
74328.body:
----------
_**Your Weekly Deep Dive into The World of Crypto**_

**June 10, 2026 · ****5 Min Read**

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/4bfe554f-a72e-4d33-9465-7cdfa4e38fb6/image.png?t=1781097727)
Caption: **Market Snapshot  · As of June 10****_  |  _****BTC Price ****_ |  _****YTD Return ****_ |  _****Fear & Greed ****_ |  _****ETF Flows (Weekly) ****_ _**

## 📡** THIS WEEK IN CRYPTO**

**GM, Readers!**

If last week felt like crypto stubbed its toe, this week was a full-body face-plant.

Bitcoin lost $12,000 in ten days. The ghost of Mt. Gox stirred for the first time in months, $1.1 billion in leveraged longs got vaporized in a single Friday session, and Strategy and BitMine were sitting on a combined $23 billion in paper losses by Saturday. The Fear & Greed Index hit 8, the lowest reading since the FTX collapse.

_**Crypto did not have a week. The week had crypto.**_

_**Let’s get into it...**_


----------View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/6c53930f-b4d7-4e7d-baee-6a88bb56a6da/image.png?t=1781097813)
Caption: 

----------
# 🐋 THE BIG WHALE MOVES

### **1. ****Mt. Gox Stirs After Six Months**

On June 2 at 04:47 UTC, the Mt. Gox estate _**moved 10,422 BTC, worth about $739 million**_, in a single transaction. It was the largest move from the defunct estate in months, with 10,306 BTC going to a brand-new address.

The market did not wait to see if the coins ever touched an exchange order book. Within an hour of the headline, BTC fell from $71K to $69.9K. The trustee still controls 34,500 BTC ($2.13B), with a distribution deadline of October 31, 2026.

_**The ghost of Mt. Gox does not need to sell to move markets. It only needs to move.**_

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/2a2ff27f-91a8-42d1-a103-022b5df85338/image.png?t=1781070567)
Caption: 

### **2. ****The Friday Liquidation Cascade**

June 5 was the kind of session that ends careers. BTC sliced through $63K on a hot May NFP print, and the leverage that had been quietly stacking finally found the floor. CoinDesk tracked _**$1.1 billion in liquidations over 24 hours**_, with longs accounting for 87% of the carnage.

_**For the full week, total BTC and ETH liquidations topped $2.2 billion. Forced selling does not need a reason. It just needs the wrong price.**_

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/05acf958-55db-45fd-8065-b27795bad876/image.png?t=1781070607)
Caption: 

### **3. ****Treasury Companies Underwater**

By June 6, [Lookonchain estimated](https://x.com/lookonchain/status/2063055776760168706?ref_src) that BitMine Immersion and Strategy were sitting on a combined $23.1 billion

🧠 AI's Crypto News Brief: May CPI surged to a 3-year high of 4.2%, but a cooler core reading gave Bitcoin room to bounce above $62K; simultaneously, U.S. and EU regulators each made their first moves to bring prediction markets and DeFi under formal rule frameworks. 📊 US CPI Hits 4.2% — A 3-Year High — But Bitcoin Bounces on Cooler Core Reading May's U.S. Consumer Price Index confirmed headline inflation at 4.2% year-over-year — the highest level since early 2023 — as energy costs inflated by the ongoing Iran conflict pushed prices higher. The market-moving detail, however, was the core reading: stripping out food and energy, monthly inflation came in at 0.2%, slightly below the 0.3% feared by traders. Read full story ⚖️ CFTC Issues First-Ever U.S. Rule Proposal for Prediction Markets The Commodity Futures Trading Commission proposed the first formal federal rulemaking for prediction market contracts, opening a public comment period on a framework that would assess whether individual contracts serve the "public interest." The move arrives the same week Kalshi — facing insider-trading scrutiny — rolled out employer-disclosure requirements for traders in high-risk markets. More details 🇪🇺 EU Opens Consultation to Expand MiCA to Cover DeFi, Crypto Perps & Prediction Markets The European Commission has launched a formal consultation on extending MiCA's regulatory perimeter to asset classes deliberately left out of the original framework — including decentralized finance protocols, tokenized perpetual derivatives, and prediction markets. The consultation signals the EU is entering MiCA's second, more ambitious phase, with sweeping potential implications for DeFi protocols, onchain derivatives venues, and prediction platforms operating under European jurisdiction. Read more 🏦 Japan's Three Largest Banks Plan a Joint Yen Stablecoin — Launch Target: March 2027 MUFG Bank, Mizuho Bank, and Sumitomo Mitsui Banking Corporation have signed a memorandum of understanding to jointly issue a yen-denominated stablecoin targeting commercial launch during fiscal year 2026 — no later than March 2027. The trio collectively dominate Japan's corporate and retail banking sector. See details 💰 Fold Sells $45M in Bitcoin to Eliminate All Debt — Stock Surges 160% Nasdaq-listed Bitcoin fintech firm Fold disclosed it liquidated approximately $45 million i

74325.subject: Anthropic reclaims the AI crown with Mythos
74325.body:
The Atlas

Автор: GenAI Works


Автор: GenAI Works

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/anthropic-reclaims-ai-crown-mythos-genai-works-dt3pc?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BLDYQpHbVSCejvxlvYsFXjw%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=15p2htv5KvZsg1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mq89or4x~1q-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mq89or4x-1q
  
Welcome back! The model Anthropic said it would not release to the public just shipped, but the real story is bigger than one launch: AI is moving from answering questions to operating inside the systems we trust. Anthropic split one frontier model into public and restricted versions, Siri is becoming screen-aware and personal-context aware, NotebookLM is turning into a cloud workspace for research, and a healthcare study shows AI triage can change when one demographic word changes. Four stories, one signal: the next AI race is not only about capability, but about access, control, context, and trust. In today's Atlas Newsletter: Anthropic shipped the model it said it never would: one model splits into two, and the fallback trick behind the safe version. How Siri finally reads your screen: Apple rebuilds Siri around context, on the device and in the cloud. NotebookLM can write and run its own code: Google hands every notebook a cloud computer and eight export formats. Your AI doctor is gender profiling you: change one word in the symptoms and the urgency collapses. PS: Want to collaborate with the biggest GTM engine and distribution moat for AI companies? You can feature your tool with us and reach millions. Anthropic shipped the model it said it never would How does one AI model become two? The answer reveals one of the most interesting safety architectures we have seen in frontier AI so far. Anthropic has launched Claude Fable 5, a Mythos-class model that is now available to the public. That is significant because Anthropic previously argued that models with this level of capability should not be broadly released until adequate safeguards existed to manage their risks. The challenge was straightforward. The same capabilities that help security teams find vulnerabilities can also help attackers exploit them. The same scientific reasoning that accelerates research can potentially be applied to dangerous biological or chemical problems. Anthropic's solution redirects risky requests rather than blocking them. When users ask questions in sensitive areas such as cybersecurity, biology, chemistry or model distillation, a separate layer of AI classifiers evaluates the request. If a risk threshold is triggered, the task is automatically handed off to Claude Opus 4.8 instead of Fable 5. The user still receives an answer, just from the less powerful model. According
